jokes.text: If you weren't all the way on the other side of the room, I'd slap your face.
jokes.setup: Michael calling Lindsay lazy for not cleaning up after fumigation
jokes.punchline: Too lazy to even walk across room to slap her
jokes.explanation: Efficient character moment showing Michael's frustration and Lindsay's laziness in one line

jokes.text: Michael had asked her to do this, but that was long before he began hiding his father in the attic.
jokes.setup: Lindsay complaining about cleaning request
jokes.punchline: Reveals Michael's priorities have shifted to hiding fugitive father
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator reveal that recontextualizes the entire domestic dispute

jokes.text: What the...? Oh, God! Polly, cover your mouth!
jokes.setup: Fumigation happening with family unaware George Sr. is in attic
jokes.punchline: George Sr. trying to protect parrot from poison gas meant to kill insects
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy from hiding scenario, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: She hadn't even done it that time, but instead, intercepted her mother's housekeeper
jokes.setup: Michael saying Lindsay cleaned once
jokes.punchline: She didn't even do it then - stole credit from intercepting Lupe
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Lindsay's extreme laziness and deception

jokes.text: Lupe never accepted a ride from Lindsay again.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's elaborate deception to avoid cleaning
jokes.punchline: Understatement of Lupe's reasonable reaction
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator understatement button on the sequence

jokes.text: I did notice we'd stopped TiVoing the Christopher Lowell show. What happened?
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking if Michael noticed Tobias moved out
jokes.punchline: Only noticed absence through domestic TV viewing patterns
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's obliviousness to family dynamics

jokes.text: You're going to stretch them out. That's all you can say? Well, excuse me for liking the way they shape my junk.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying on Lindsay's underwear
jokes.punchline: His casual defense of cross-dressing for comfort
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias obliviousness to social norms, perfectly in character

jokes.text: You can pack your junk and get out.
jokes.setup: Tobias talking about his 'junk' in underwear
jokes.punchline: Lindsay using his word choice in kicking him out
jokes.explanation: Efficient wordplay callback that serves story function

jokes.text: At the beginning of pilot season?
jokes.setup: Tobias being kicked out of house
jokes.punchline: His concern is about TV pilot season timing, not homelessness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character priorities - more worried about career than marriage

jokes.text: took up residence in the apartment of Detective Frank Wrench on the set of the television show: Wrench.
jokes.setup: Tobias needs somewhere to live
jokes.punchline: Living in fake TV apartment on soundstage
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation that's perfectly logical for Tobias's delusions

jokes.text: Did he take the good video camera with him?
jokes.setup: Family discussing Tobias moving out
jokes.punchline: Gob's only concern is material possessions
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob selfishness in emotional family moment

jokes.text: I'm calling it Tricks... let me finish-- Around the Office.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his magic video concept
jokes.punchline: Defensive about obvious title being interrupted
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob's pride in obvious ideas

jokes.text: That is what we call working.
jokes.setup: Gob saying he'll make money while working
jokes.punchline: Michael pointing out that's just 'working'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of Gob's 'brilliant' plan

jokes.text: Is that Qusay Hussein at the omelet bar?
jokes.setup: Watching company anniversary party video
jokes.punchline: Casual identification of war criminal at corporate event
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about company's corrupt international dealings

jokes.text: George Michael had been heavily influenced by the Star Wars films, and had endeavored to recreate his favorite moments.
jokes.setup: Family watching embarrassing home video
jokes.punchline: Teenager's earnest Star Wars reenactment on tape
jokes.explanation: Relatable teenage embarrassment played for family cringe

jokes.text: Even you have to admit that that was the lamest thing ever put on tape. Not so.
jokes.setup: Calling George Michael's video the lamest ever
jokes.punchline: Narrator promising something even worse exists
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Buster callback, creates anticipation

jokes.text: Buster had once videotaped himself recreating moments from his then favorite film, Chicago.
jokes.setup: Promise of something lamer than Star Wars reenactment
jokes.punchline: Buster doing Chicago musical numbers
jokes.explanation: Perfect character callback that escalates the embarrassment while being completely in character for Buster

jokes.text: He also liked Star Wars.
jokes.setup: Buster's Chicago obsession
jokes.punchline: Casual addition that he mixed in Star Wars too
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - suggests even more embarrassing mashup content

jokes.text: That one gets pregnant, it stays pregnant. Believe me, I dated a chick like that once in high school. No, I didn't.
jokes.setup: Gob making crude comment about religious girls
jokes.punchline: Immediately retracting his false bravado
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - false confidence immediately undercut by honesty

jokes.text: Don't start. Smoking. It's a killer. That Sammy Davis, Jr. should still be performing.
jokes.setup: Michael overhearing 'Don't start' conversation
jokes.punchline: Ann's anti-smoking PSA with odd Sammy Davis Jr. reference
jokes.explanation: Good misdirect that shows Ann's earnest but awkward personality

jokes.text: You are George Michael! Who's cooler than you?
jokes.setup: George Michael saying only cool kids win elections
jokes.punchline: Michael's earnest belief his dorky son is cool
jokes.explanation: Sweet but deluded parental support, establishes Michael's blind spot

jokes.text: I said, 'Let's, do not... Let's not put the school through that.' You know, I haven't thought about that for years.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he can't remember high school election
jokes.punchline: Detailed 'memory' of his magnanimous response to losing
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Michael's self-serving memory

jokes.text: And then that first time, after waiting and waiting, it's gonna be so awesome! Because it's not just gonna be my love and George Michael's, but God's love, as well. God, it's gonna be incredible!
jokes.setup: Ann talking about waiting for marriage
jokes.punchline: Increasingly sexual enthusiasm about divine threesome
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of religious rhetoric into inappropriate territory

jokes.text: Oh, God! Oh, God!
jokes.setup: Buster trying to use prosthetic hand for medical work
jokes.punchline: Clearly struggling with basic manipulation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy from Buster's disability, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Unfortunately, it was a new friend to George, Senior. Polly!
jokes.setup: Lindsay discovering eBay as 'old friend'
jokes.punchline: George Sr. panicking about parrot discovering computer
jokes.explanation: Good parallel structure contrasting Lindsay's discovery with George Sr.'s panic

jokes.text: Sometimes it's as simple as turning 10:45 in the morning into... lunch time!
jokes.setup: Gob explaining office magic
jokes.punchline: Dramatic reveal of basic time passage
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob delusion - treating mundane events as magic

jokes.text: Well, you kind of have to, guy. I'm the president. No, no. I'm the president.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to fire Gob
jokes.punchline: Confusion over who holds what office
jokes.explanation: Sibling rivalry over corporate hierarchy, moderately effective

jokes.text: Pound is tic-tac-toe, right? Yeah, pound is this one here.
jokes.setup: Gob trying to call security to fire Michael
jokes.punchline: Can't figure out basic phone operation
jokes.explanation: Standard incompetence gag, though in character for Gob

jokes.text: But the guard had recently lost half his tie to office magic.
jokes.setup: Security guard willing to escort Michael out
jokes.punchline: Motivated by Gob's earlier magic trick going wrong
jokes.explanation: Nice payoff to earlier setup, explains guard's motivation

jokes.text: Your Uncle Gob doesn't not-work here anymore.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking about office situation
jokes.punchline: Michael's confused double negative about Gob's employment
jokes.explanation: Verbal confusion that reflects Michael's own confusion about the situation

jokes.text: Marry me! I'm so sorry. I forgot who I was talking to.
jokes.setup: Ann excited about campaign posters
jokes.punchline: Accidentally proposes to Michael, then realizes mistake
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Ann's enthusiasm and subsequent embarrassment

jokes.text: And he's won three years in a row. Every year after his first junior year.
jokes.setup: Describing Steve Holt's election dominance
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of him being held back
jokes.explanation: Efficient reveal of Steve's academic failures disguised as success

jokes.text: Well, that's like comparing apples and some fruit nobody's ever heard of.
jokes.setup: Comparing Steve Holt's popularity to George Michael's
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment disguised as helpful analogy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of cliché phrase to deliver devastating comparison

jokes.text: You can't fire me. I'm your son. I'm firing you! I was firing Lupe.
jokes.setup: Confusion over who's being fired
jokes.punchline: Multiple people firing each other simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of family dysfunction with efficient resolution

jokes.text: I no does Buster anymore.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking Lupe to continue working
jokes.punchline: Lupe's broken English refusing Buster specifically
jokes.explanation: Character-specific speech pattern creates double meaning

jokes.text: Also, I don't think this is a real toilet.
jokes.setup: Tobias calling from set bathroom
jokes.punchline: Discovering TV set bathroom isn't functional
jokes.explanation: Logical consequence of living on TV set that Tobias discovers too late

jokes.text: You're a dirty cop, so Frank Wrench is going to clean you.
jokes.setup: Tobias hiding in TV show bathroom
jokes.punchline: Show dialogue about 'cleaning' criminals while Tobias needs actual cleaning
jokes.explanation: Clever layering of show dialogue with Tobias's actual situation

jokes.text: Four more years! Four more years!
jokes.setup: George Michael getting excited about running
jokes.punchline: Using presidential re-election chant for student government
jokes.explanation: Endearing overenthusiasm with inappropriate political reference

jokes.text: It's a robot. I hate it. I miss Lupe.
jokes.setup: Lucille showing off robot replacement for Lupe
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret and longing for fired employee
jokes.explanation: Efficient character moment showing Lucille's impulsive decisions and immediate regret

jokes.text: It's a robot. I hate it. I miss Lupe.
jokes.setup: Lucille introducing her new robotic housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Immediate hatred and nostalgia for human help
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lucille's immediate regret. The economy is perfect - three short sentences tell the whole story.

jokes.text: My name is Phyllida Featherbottom, and I can cook and I can clean and I can take care of the little ones.
jokes.setup: New housekeeper at the door
jokes.punchline: Obviously Tobias in drag with terrible accent
jokes.explanation: Mrs. Doubtfire parody setup, relies on recognition but fits character perfectly

jokes.text: When you put a squirt of frosting down your throat before we take our medications...
jokes.setup: Tobias singing as Mary Poppins-style nanny
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate lyrics about pills and frosting
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on wholesome nanny songs, perfectly uncomfortable

jokes.text: It was the exact plot of the film Mrs. Doubtfire. There was also some Mary Poppins in there.
jokes.setup: Tobias's elaborate disguise plan
jokes.punchline: Narrator acknowledging the obvious movie references
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary acknowledges the derivative nature, makes it intentional

jokes.text: From Blackstool. I'm charmed, I'm sure.
jokes.setup: Introducing housekeeper's background
jokes.punchline: Crude fake location name with overly formal response
jokes.explanation: Juvenile place name humor, though the formal response adds character

jokes.text: You and Gob were like those biblical brothers, Gallant and, um... Goofuth.
jokes.setup: George Sr. making biblical comparison
jokes.punchline: Confusing children's magazine with Bible, making up 'Goofuth'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant confusion of cultural references creating absurd biblical name

jokes.text: See, the mere fact that you think you can says to me that I shouldn't let you.
jokes.setup: Gob offering to help with election campaign
jokes.punchline: Michael's circular logic about Gob's confidence
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic showing Michael's conflicted feelings about Gob's abilities

jokes.text: What do you expect, Mother? I'm half machine! I'm a monster!
jokes.setup: Robot maid not working properly
jokes.punchline: Buster's melodramatic identification with broken machinery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster dramatics connecting his prosthetic to the broken robot

jokes.text: We hand out rubbers that say, 'We salute George Michael.'
jokes.setup: Gob suggesting campaign strategy
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate condom giveaway idea
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob inappropriate suggestion that misses the point entirely

jokes.text: 2%, huh? Is that how little I lost by? Lost by? It was close. That's all you got.
jokes.setup: Gob calling Michael 'Two Percent'
jokes.punchline: Michael fixating on the exact margin of his high school loss
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Michael's lingering sensitivity about old failure

jokes.text: I'm sure wherever your father is right now, she loves you very, very much.
jokes.setup: Tobias as nanny talking to Maeby about her father
jokes.punchline: Gender confusion while being the absent father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Tobias unknowingly counseling his own daughter about his absence while in disguise

jokes.text: Drought, starvation... these are the things my family has had to deal with for generations, although, fortunately, not since we moved to Corona del Mar.
jokes.setup: Seemingly serious speech about hardship
jokes.punchline: Reveals privileged Southern California upbringing
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirect subverting expectations about immigrant hardship narrative

jokes.text: Go home, you terrorist!
jokes.setup: Indian student's heartfelt campaign speech
jokes.punchline: Racist heckling from student audience
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about prejudice, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: This kid's popular? Looks like a chick I did once in high school.
jokes.setup: Watching Steve Holt's campaign video
jokes.punchline: Gob's crude assessment and boast
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob crudeness that will have major payoff later in series

jokes.text: Steve Holt is a bastard. He doesn't even know who his real father is.
jokes.setup: Attack ad against Steve Holt
jokes.punchline: Using literal definition of 'bastard' as attack
jokes.explanation: Clever use of word's dual meaning for political attack, sets up series-long arc

jokes.text: The girls like him just fine. Young and old, it doesn't matter... in the dark.
jokes.setup: Positive campaign message about George Michael
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate implications about age and darkness
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable escalation that makes innocent campaign sound creepy

jokes.text: Uh-oh... I couldn't find another tape.
jokes.setup: Embarrassing campaign video
jokes.punchline: Forced to show the Star Wars footage
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier embarrassment creates maximum cringe moment

jokes.text: No, I tied with 'Bart Simpson' and 'School Sucks.'
jokes.setup: George Michael saying he did better than his dad
jokes.punchline: Tied with joke write-in candidates
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of any pride in beating his father's record

jokes.text: George Michael... was that really you doing those light saber moves? You were incredible.
jokes.setup: Expected mockery after embarrassing video
jokes.punchline: Genuine admiration for the Star Wars skills
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - embarrassment becomes source of admiration

jokes.text: Yoo-hoo! I'll take you the rest of the way!
jokes.setup: Episode ending with various resolutions
jokes.punchline: Lindsay still trying to trick people into cleaning
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Lindsay learned nothing from earlier experience

jokes.text: Well, we got some bad news. Wow. Is that what's going to happen to my hair?
jokes.setup: Steve Holt getting DNA results about his father
jokes.punchline: More concerned about inherited baldness than identity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Steve's vanity over deeper concerns, sets up series arc
